touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Carter Le introducing AskElephant's onboarding automation capabilities for PropTech companies. No response received.
LinkedIn message to Carter Le highlighting how AskElephant helps RevOps teams automate repetitive onboarding workflows. Carter responded expressing interest in learning more.

questions_asked.question: Do you work with companies our size in PropTech?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have several PropTech clients in the 50-100 employee range using our platform for onboarding automation.

touches.ai_summary: 11-minute qualification call with Carter Le to understand BirchAI's onboarding challenges. Carter described manual processes causing delays in customer activation. Qualified fit for unlimited automation tier.

questions_asked.question: What's your current onboarding process like?
questions_asked.answer: Very manual - lots of data entry, document collection, and follow-ups. Takes 2-3 weeks per customer.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can we see results?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see 40-60% time savings within the first month of implementation.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call to dive deeper into workflows

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute discovery call exploring BirchAI's onboarding workflow in detail. Carter walked through current state processes, pain points with manual data collection, and goals to reduce onboarding time by half. Discussed team structure - 4 seats needed for RevOps team.

questions_asked.question: Who else is involved in the onboarding process?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly our RevOps team - 4 people total. Customer Success hands off to us after the sale.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling faster than expected and the manual process is becoming a bottleneck. Customer complaints about slow activation.

questions_asked.question: Can this integrate with our existing CRM and data warehouse?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with major CRMs and can connect to most data warehouses via API.

next_steps: Prepare demo focused on document collection and data entry automation
Carter to map out ideal state workflow

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email recapping discovery call key points and sharing relevant case study from similar-sized PropTech company. Carter replied confirming demo time and asking about implementation timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks from contract signing to first workflows live, depending on integration complexity.

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute demo showcasing document collection automation, data entry workflows, and customer communication sequences. Carter was engaged and asked detailed questions about customization. Showed strong interest in unlimited automation tier for flexibility as they scale.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the workflow logic ourselves or do we need your team?
questions_asked.answer: You can build and modify workflows yourself with our visual builder - no coding required. Our team helps with initial setup.

questions_asked.question: How does the unlimited automation tier differ from the standard plan?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited gives you no caps on workflow executions, advanced conditional logic, and priority support - ideal for high-volume use cases.

questions_asked.question: Does this demo match what you had in mind?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, this is exactly what we need. I'd like our Operations Manager to see this in action too.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation with Operations team member

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute technical evaluation with Carter Le and Sarah Kim (Operations Manager). Deep dive into API capabilities, data security, workflow builder, and integration requirements. Sarah asked detailed technical questions about data handling and compliance. Both seemed satisfied with technical capabilities.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle PII and what compliance certifications do you have?
questions_asked.answer: We're SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant. All data encrypted in transit and at rest, with granular access controls.

questions_asked.question: Can we export our workflow configurations for version control?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, workflows can be exported as JSON and we have a version history feature built into the platform.

questions_asked.question: Are there any technical blockers you see for moving forward?
questions_asked.answer: No blockers from my end - the integrations look solid and security meets our requirements.

questions_asked.question: What level of support comes with the unlimited tier?
questions_asked.answer: Priority email and chat support with 4-hour SLA during business hours, plus quarterly business reviews.

objections: We'll need to loop in our IT team for final security sign-off, but that's usually just a formality.
next_steps: Carter to prepare internal business case
Schedule proposal call to review pricing

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute proposal call reviewing pricing for 4 seats on unlimited automation tier ($4,200 ACV). Carter seemed hesitant about budget approval process and mentioned needing to get VP of Operations buy-in, though VP hasn't been involved in conversations yet.

questions_asked.question: What's your approval process for a purchase of this size?
questions_asked.answer: I have budget authority up to $5K, but I usually run these by my VP to keep her in the loop. She trusts my judgment on tools.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on annual vs quarterly payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: We typically do annual contracts but can discuss quarterly payment schedule if that helps with cash flow.

questions_asked.question: When would you ideally want to go live?
questions_asked.answer: As soon as possible - ideally within the next 4-6 weeks.

objections: The price is a bit higher than I was expecting. I need to make sure this fits our Q2 budget.
next_steps: Carter to review internally and discuss with VP

touches.ai_summary: 17-minute negotiation call. Carter requested 15% discount to fit budget constraints. Discussed payment terms and contract start date. Carter confirmed he's still working on getting time with VP but expects it to be straightforward approval.

questions_asked.question: Can you do $3,600 for the first year to help us get this approved?
questions_asked.answer: I can offer 10% off for annual prepayment, bringing it to $3,780. That's the best I can do while maintaining the unlimited tier benefits you need.

questions_asked.question: Have you had a chance to brief your VP on this?
questions_asked.answer: I sent her an email overview. She's traveling this week but I'm confident she'll support this - it directly addresses our customer satisfaction issues.

objections: I really need to hit $3,600 to make the budget math work cleanly.
next_steps: Rep to check with management on additional discount flexibility
Carter to schedule brief alignment call with VP

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email confirming approved pricing of $3,780 (10% discount) with annual prepayment terms. Sent contract for review. Carter acknowledged receipt and said he'd have contract reviewed internally this week, still working on VP's calendar availability.
next_steps: Carter to complete internal contract review
Target contract signature by end of week
